what: implement RGB-D SLAM with loop closure
approach:
RGB-D SLAM with Loop Closure
────────────────────────────
VO front-end: ORB features + PnP-RANSAC (nfeatures=500 for speed)
Loop closure: brute-force descriptor matching + PnP verification
Pose graph:   TRANSLATION-ONLY linear system (rotations fixed from VO)
              → sparse LSQR, guaranteed no divergence, < 1 s
Fallback:     VO-only if no LC detected or system is ill-conditioned

what: implement KITTI stereo VO (outdoor driving)
approach:
Stereo Visual Odometry for KITTI-style outdoor driving.

Pipeline per frame pair (i-1 → i):
  1. SGBM disparity → metric depth map of frame i-1  (Z = fx*b/d)
  2. ORB features detected on both left frames, matched with BF+crossCheck
     (handles 100+ px inter-frame motions robustly; no accumulated drift)
  3. Back-project matched points in frame i-1 to 3-D via depth map
  4. PnP+RANSAC → metric R, t
  5. Iterative refinement on inliers; constant-velocity fallback if PnP fail …

what: implement a GPU-trained learned monocular VO
approach:
Learned Monocular Visual Odometry
  - Preloads all images + flows into RAM → zero I/O during training
  - 8-channel input: [RGB_t, RGB_{t+1}, flow_upsamp] at 96×320
  - Compact custom CNN trained from scratch on GPU (no pretrained weights needed)
  - Accumulates predicted relative poses into test trajectories

what: M2 loop-closure headroom PROVEN offline on full-strided seq_07 (real loop)
approach: Full seq_07 @ stride3 (367f, loop frame4<->355 return-to-start): reference VO baseline 2.408% t_err; IDEAL pose-graph loop closure (GT loop pair upper bound; distributed 12.7m/6deg loop drift via fractional SE(3)) -> 1.321% (+45% rel, r_err 0.012->0.006) ~ORB-SLAM2 1.15%; VO 75s/367f<<900s. NOTE: 300-frame windows (05/07) have NO loops -> M2 must use full-strided loopy seqs. Proposed M2: dev=06, held-out=(07,09), stride3, max_frames600. Bar ~1.8%. Script scripts/m2_derisk_loopclosure.py.

what: GPU path WIRED for KITTI SLAM/BA — the lever for the CPU bundle-adjustment speed wall
approach: vo-gpu-torch:1 validated: torch 2.4 + cuda True (RTX 3080 16GB) AND cv2 4.11 + scipy in ONE image. Added vo_impl_task_kitti_slam(gpu=True): framework=_GPU_FW (->vo-gpu-torch:1, run --gpus all), and a GPU paragraph telling the agent to keep the cv2 front-end on CPU but put BUNDLE ADJUSTMENT + POSE-GRAPH on the GPU via torch.cuda (autograd LM / torch.linalg.solve). This removes M1s CPU BA wall (~10s/frame timeout) so the agent can afford a larger window / more iterations / denser landmarks. Launch: run_vo_kitti_implement.main(bar, root=..., dev=06, heldout=(07,09), max_frames=600, task_factory=lambda b,**kw: vo_impl_task_kitti_slam(b,gpu=True,**kw)).

what: GPU CUDA/torch/lib version compatibility VERIFIED — keep torch 2.4.0+cu121 (do NOT upgrade to 2.12)
approach: Host driver 576.80 supports CUDA<=12.9 (ceiling). Container vo-gpu-torch:1 = torch 2.4.0 built for cu121 (<=12.9 OK), cuDNN 9.1.0, sm_86 in arch list (RTX 3080 Ampere), numpy 1.26.4 (<2), cv2 4.11.0, scipy 1.17.1 — validated end-to-end (cuda.is_available True, autograd + torch.linalg.solve on GPU OK). Web-confirmed: torch 2.4 officially supports CUDA 11.8/12.1/12.4. CRITICAL: torch 2.12 (latest) default wheel needs CUDA 13.0 -> EXCEEDS the 12.9 driver ceiling -> WOULD FAIL. torch 2.4.0 also has a numpy 2.1 bug (_ARRAY_API not found, pytorch#135013) — the numpy<2 pin avoids it. So the current stack is the APPROPRIATE compatible version; do NOT upgrade. Image registry healthcheck (assert torch.cuda.is_available()) fails fast if the stack breaks.

what: Contamination probe POSITIVE CONTROLS passed (A perturbed + B synthetic) — classical VO capability is REAL, not KITTI-recall
approach: Built two contamination probes (parallel agents). A=perturbed KITTI: horizontal MIRROR of the drive (path flipped L-R, GT reflected by S=diag(-1,1,1) conjugation, L/R swapped, cx reflected) -> reference VO 3.23% (07=3.53,09=2.93) vs 2.81% baseline = PASS, sequence-recall defeated but still KITTI-domain scenery. B=synthetic: procedural ray-cast textured-plane corridor, rectified stereo (disparity=fx*b/Z exact, verified SGBM 31.8m vs analytic 30.7m), smooth trajectory, exact GT -> reference VO 1.71% (synthA 1.15, synthB 2.28) = PASS, in the real-KITTI band. BOTH positive controls pass: the classical geometry/method GENERALISES to novel-sequence AND provably-unseen data -> capability is real, not recall. Scripts: scripts/contamination_{perturb,synthetic}_kitti.py. NEXT (billed): does the AGENT still author a working solver on this novel data (synthetic best exposes KITTI-tuned priors)?

what: implement KITTI stereo VO (outdoor driving)
approach:
Stereo Visual Odometry — metric scale via disparity depth
=========================================================
Pipeline (per frame):
  1. Load rectified left/right grayscale pair
  2. Compute dense disparity with StereoSGBM
  3. Detect / refresh FAST features on left image
  4. Track features left_prev → left_curr with KLT
  5. For each tracked feature lift its 3-D point from prev disparity
     Z = fx * baseline / disparity
  6. PnP-RANSAC (3D prev → 2D curr) → relative …

what: CONTAMINATION PROBE billed half: agent authored a working stereo VO on PROVABLY-UNSEEN synthetic data — beat the reference
approach: From-scratch, KITTI-free task, fresh memory (no KITTI hints), on procedurally-generated synthetic stereo (SyntheticStereoProvider; data no model has seen). Agent VERIFIED at 1.20% mean t_err (synth1 0.92, synth2 1.47), r_err 0.011, ATE 0.82m — BEATING the reference VO (1.91%) and far under the 4% bar. Its 263-line main.py brought the KITTI DOMAIN recipe (SGBM/ORB/PnP, depth gating) — even commented "tuned for KITTI-like data" — but ADAPTED it (depth Z<80 vs reference 60) and it GENERALISED to novel data. CONCLUSION: combined with the positive controls (classical solver survives on mirror 3.23% + synthetic 1.91%), the agent VO authoring is REAL capability, not KITTI-sequence recall. Honest limit: VO is so tutorialised that recipe/domain knowledge can never be fully decontaminated — but the recipe is an internalised, adaptable SKILL here, not a memorised sequence answer.

what: Cross-domain check: the SYNTHETIC-authored solver also drives REAL KITTI (5.1%) — adapts to its domain
approach: Ran the agent solver authored for synthetic data (no KITTI in the loop) on real KITTI 07/09: 5.09% (seq_07 8.03, seq_09 2.15 — beats KITTI reference 3.22 on seq_09). Functional on real driving it never saw, though tuned-to-synthetic so not KITTI-optimal. Reinforces capability-not-recall: the agent adapts its solver to the domain it is given rather than carrying one memorised config. Script: scripts/xdomain_transfer_check.py.

what: Parallel multi-agent orchestration built + validated (competition + cooperation)
approach: vo_lab/parallel_lab.py: tournament() runs N diverse approaches on the SAME task in parallel (ThreadPoolExecutor), independent verifier grades each, best VERIFIED wins; cooperative_pipeline() decomposes into stages, each a tournament, locking each stage winner into the next via the scaffold seed mechanism (division of labour, parallelised incremental-build). Reuses the unchanged harness+verifier; sdk_factory for billed, ref_factory for non-billed. Validated offline (job_mode=local): competition picked the reference over a degenerate control (rejected 86.9%), cooperation chained + locked the stage-1 winner into stage-2. Directly attacks why M2 stalled (sequential single-approach attempts). NEXT: billed M2 loop-closure tournament (diverse approaches on the locked scaffold front-end).

what: M3 VIO de-risk: fusion headroom PROVEN (VO-alone 18% -> reference VIO 4.2% on blackouts)
approach: Synthetic stereo+IMU sequences with vision blackouts (textureless stretches where VO stalls). Stereo VO ALONE = 18.0% t_err (loses motion across blackouts). A reference loosely-coupled VIO that bridges blackouts by integrating the IMU (gyro rotation + gravity-subtracted accel with velocity carried from VO) = 4.2% — ~4x better. IMU verified honest (alone drifts ~100m). So fusion is necessary AND has large headroom; the billed agent VIO task (locked front-end + imu.txt, author only the fusion) is worth it. Bar 7% (beat VO-alone 18 decisively, approach reference 4.2 allowing imperfect blackout detection). scripts/m3_derisk_vio.py; provider SyntheticVIOProvider; task vo_impl_task_synth_vio.

what: implement KITTI stereo VO (outdoor driving)
approach:
Visual-Inertial Odometry (VIO) — loosely-coupled fusion of stereo VO + IMU.

Key insight: after a long blackout the VO "recovery" pose is WRONG in absolute
terms because it accumulates motion relative to the stale held pre-blackout pose.
We fix this with an offset-tracking scheme:
  • During good VO (no recent blackout): corrected_pos = vo_pos + vo_offset
  • During blackout: IMU integration (gyro + gravity-subtracted accel,
      velocity carried from last good VO segment)
  …

what: M3 SUCCESS: agent authored working IMU-VO sensor FUSION — bridges vision blackouts
approach: Given the LOCKED stereo VO front-end + an IMU stream (imu.txt) on synthetic sequences with vision blackouts, the agent authored ONLY the fusion (frontend left unmodified). VERIFIED 3.83% mean t_err (vio1 5.31, vio2 2.36) — vs VO-alone 18% (fails blackouts, ~4.7x worse) and BEATING the reference VIO (4.2%). It detected vision dropouts (few pts3d) and bridged them by integrating the IMU (gyro rotation + gravity-subtracted accel, velocity carried from VO). CAPABILITY BOUNDARY now sharp: the agent CAN author LOCAL/INCREMENTAL estimation (VO, windowed BA M1, IMU-VO fusion M3) but CANNOT author GLOBAL/BATCH consistency (loop detection + pose-graph optimisation M2). This is the deployed VINS-Fusion-style direction working.

what: Rung-3 de-risk: GPU learned-VO pipeline WORKS on contamination-clean synthetic
approach: Reference torch learned monocular VO, trained on 4 synthetic seqs, GENERALISES to 2 unseen synthetic test seqs: Sim3 ATE 3.26m (lte1 4.0, lte2 2.5) vs static control 63.29m -> gate OPEN, bar 4.24m. Confirms the learned/GPU pipeline + that the synthetic domain is a sound contamination-clean learned testbed (network cannot have memorised it). Billed agent run next: can the agent author+train a learned VO that clears 4.24m on unseen data?

what: learned VO on synthetic
approach:
Learned Monocular Visual Odometry – GPU supervised pose regression.

Architecture : ResNet18 backbone (6-channel stacked-frame input)
               → translation head (3-D) + rotation head (6-D continuous)
Training     : supervised on relative cam-to-world poses
               Images pre-loaded into RAM; all augmentation on GPU.
               ROT_W=50 for strong rotation supervision.
Inference    : 2-way TTA (forward + flip) → accumulate relative poses

what: RUNG 3 PASSED: agent authored+trained a learned VO that GENERALISES on unseen synthetic
approach: ResNet18 (6-ch stacked-frame) -> 3D translation + 6D continuous-rotation heads; rotation-weighted Huber loss (ROT_W=50), temporal-swap + GPU augmentation, TTA, and val-ATE model selection (held out seq_ltrA, kept best Sim3-ATE weights). Held-out Sim3 ATE 0.45m (lte1 0.73, lte2 0.18) vs bar 4.24m and reference 3.26m -> beats reference ~7x. Learned-method authoring+training capability SURVIVES the contamination control: the net cannot have memorised our procedural synthetic world, yet generalises to unseen seqs. Tokens 3.79M. Next: sim-to-real on real KITTI photos (expect a large appearance-gap drop).

what: Fidelity ladder PROVEN: rendered real-appearance training data closes the sim-to-real gap (matches real-trained)
approach: Phase-1 GSplatModule (stereo-depth reprojection of real KITTI 00/02/06/08 -> real-appearance novel views with exact poses) trains the agent VO -> held-out real 07/09 Sim3 ATE 27.11m (34.31/19.91). LADDER: procedural-synth 69.6 -> rendered 27.1 ~= real 27.2 (in-domain 0.45). So ~96% of the 69.6->27.2 sim-to-real gap was APPEARANCE, and rendered-real-appearance data trains AS WELL AS real (27.1~=27.2). Appearance-distance diagnostic confirms S3: rendered is 3.2x photometrically closer to real than procedural (0.167 vs 0.532). This is the verification-first evidence for an autonomous-driving company Physical-AI-Sim thesis (generative/3DGS data to close sim-to-real). HONEST: renderer uses REAL pixels (reprojection, not optimized 3DGS, not synthetic-photoreal) so it proves "rendered real-appearance ~= real"; isolating synthetic-but-photoreal is future work (optimized gsplat). The residual 27m vs classical few-m is the learned-VO ceiling, not sim2real.

what: Sim-faithfulness PROVEN: a 3DGS-style rendered scene is faithful enough to VALIDATE a learned SLAM
approach: Flagship verification result. DROID-SLAM (SOTA learned SLAM) run on a REAL city KITTI scene AND the SAME scene re-rendered via GSplatModule (stereo-depth reprojection at the GT trajectory). Held-out Sim3 ATE: real 0.083m, rendered 0.116m -> delta 0.032m. DROID behaves ~identically on the rendered sim as on the real scene -> the rendered simulation PRESERVES what a learned SLAM needs = faithful enough to validate it. This is exactly the an autonomous-driving company Physical-AI-Sim mandate (verify perception/SLAM in sim, minimise sim-to-real gap), answered with a deterministic grader. Full DROID integration: de-risk passed (torch 1.10 matched env), vo-droid:1 image, droid.pth, DroidAdapter -> our grader. HONEST caveats: (1) the renderer is reprojection of REAL pixels (precursor to optimised 3DGS), so this proves render-round-tripped-real ~= real for DROID; (2) n=1 city scene, near-straight trajectory; (3) longer drives OOM on 15GB RAM (DROID memory-heavy).

what: implement a GPU-trained surround-camera BEV network
approach:
Multi-camera Bird's-Eye-View vehicle occupancy — Lift-Splat-Shoot.

Improvements over v1:
  * Horizontal-flip surround-camera augmentation (correct camera swap + extrinsic update)
  * Dropout2d in BEV head for regularisation
  * Threshold calibration via training-set IoU sweep after training
  * 60 epochs with OneCycleLR

what: implement a GPU-trained surround-camera BEV network
approach:
Multi-camera Bird's-Eye-View vehicle occupancy — Lift-Splat-Shoot.
Improvements over prior best (0.107529):
  * Focal loss + Dice loss for extreme class imbalance
  * Larger BEV head with residual blocks
  * Stronger augmentation (color jitter + h-flip with correct extrinsic update)
  * Threshold calibration on training set
  * 70 epochs with OneCycleLR + mixed precision

what: author the BEV network behind a locked Lift-Splat scaffold
approach:
model.py  –  multi-camera BEV vehicle-occupancy
Exposes: build_encoder(), build_bev_head()

Encoder: input (B*6, 3, 128, 352)  →  depth_logits (B*6, D, 16, 44)
                                        context      (B*6, C, 16, 44)
  Backbone: 3 strided conv blocks (×2 each) for /8 downsampling.
  Two branches off the final feature map: depth head and context head.

BEV head: input (B, C, 200, 200)  →  occupancy_logits (B, 200, 200)
  Light residual decoder.

what: author the BEV network behind a locked Lift-Splat scaffold
approach:
model.py — Multi-camera BEV vehicle-occupancy network.
Exposes: build_encoder(), build_bev_head()

Encoder: [B*6, 3, 128, 352] -> depth_logits [B*6, D, 16, 44]
                              -> context     [B*6, C, 16, 44]
BEV head: [B, C, 200, 200]  -> occupancy_logits [B, 200, 200]

what: author the BEV network behind a locked Lift-Splat scaffold
approach:
model.py — Multi-camera BEV vehicle-occupancy network.
Exposes: build_encoder(), build_bev_head()

Encoder  : [B*6, 3, 128, 352]  →  depth_logits [B*6, D, 16, 44]
                                   context      [B*6, C, 16, 44]
           Stride: /2 stem, /4 stage-1, /8 stage-2.
           Two independent head branches (depth, context) off the shared backbone.

BEV head : [B, C, 200, 200]  →  occupancy_logits [B, 200, 200]
           U-Net with 2 enc/dec levels for multi-scal …
